name: HitlClient
description: Java high-level client for HITL lifecycle management. Handles task creation, polling, artifact upload/download, refresh loops, and task closure.

parameters.name: agenticClient
parameters.description: AWS SDK v2 client for ElasticGumbyAgenticService

parameters.name: statusPollingTimeoutMinutes
parameters.description: Max minutes to wait for human submission (overload constructor)

name: HitlClient.createAndStartTask
description: Create and start a HITL task. Calls both CreateHitlTask AND StartHitlTask API operations. The requestContext is injected automatically from the client.
signature:
String createAndStartTask(CreateHitlTaskRequest request)
String createAndStartTask(CreateHitlTaskRequest request, boolean firstInChain)

parameters.name: request
parameters.description: Built with builder pattern: uxComponentId, title, description, severity, hitlTaskType, blockingType, hitlRequestArtifact, etc.

parameters.name: firstInChain
parameters.description: True for first task in chain, false for refresh iterations

example:
CreateHitlTaskRequest request = CreateHitlTaskRequest.builder()
    .uxComponentId("DynamicHITLRenderEngine")
    .title("Review Migration Plan")
    .description("Please review the proposed migration plan")
    .severity(Severity.STANDARD)
    .hitlTaskType(HitlTaskType.NORMAL)
    .blockingType(BlockingType.BLOCKING)
    .hitlRequestArtifact(HitlTaskArtifact.builder().artifactId(artifactId).build())
    .build();
String taskId = client.createAndStartTask(request);

name: HitlClient.getTask
description: Get the current state of a HITL task.

parameters.name: hitlTaskId
parameters.description: The HITL task ID to retrieve

name: HitlClient.waitForHumanSubmission
description: Wait for the human to submit their response. Blocks until task reaches SUBMITTED status. Delegates to pollUntilStatus internally.
signature: HitlTask waitForHumanSubmission(String hitlTaskId) throws InterruptedException, TimeoutException

parameters.name: hitlTaskId
parameters.description: The HITL task ID to wait for

returns.description: HitlTask with SUBMITTED status and humanArtifact().artifactId()

raises.description: If task reaches terminal status (CANCELLED, CLOSED) before SUBMITTED

example:
HitlTask task = client.waitForHumanSubmission(taskId);
String humanArtifactId = task.humanArtifact().artifactId();

name: HitlClient.pollUntilStatus
description: Poll until a HITL task reaches a specific status. Used internally by waitForHumanSubmission but can target any HitlTaskStatus.
signature: HitlTask pollUntilStatus(String hitlTaskId, HitlTaskStatus targetStatus) throws InterruptedException, TimeoutException

parameters.name: hitlTaskId
parameters.description: The HITL task ID to poll

parameters.name: targetStatus
parameters.description: The status to wait for (e.g. HitlTaskStatus.SUBMITTED, HitlTaskStatus.DELIVERED)

returns.description: HitlTask when target status is reached

raises.description: If task reaches terminal status before target

name: HitlClient.closeTask
description: Close a HITL task with a specified closure type.

parameters.name: hitlTaskId
parameters.description: The HITL task ID to close

name: HitlClient.executeWithRefresh
description: Execute HITL with automatic refresh loop and typed response deserialization. Handles: create task -> poll -> download artifact -> check QT_REFRESH -> close or refresh -> repeat. Unlike the Python SDK, returns a typed object via Jackson deserialization.
signature: <T> T executeWithRefresh(CreateHitlTaskRequest initialRequest, Function<T, CreateHitlTaskRequest> onRefresh, int maxIterations, Class<T> responseType)

parameters.name: onRefresh
parameters.description: Takes previous typed response, returns CreateHitlTaskRequest for next iteration

parameters.name: maxIterations
parameters.description: Maximum refresh iterations before forcing completion

parameters.name: responseType
parameters.description: Class for Jackson deserialization of human artifact (e.g. MyResponse.class)

returns.description: Typed human artifact response, deserialized via Jackson

name: HitlClient.uploadArtifact
description: Upload content as an artifact to the artifact store. Handles the full upload workflow: SHA256 digest, presigned URL, S3 PUT, and completion. The requestContext and contentDigest are set automatically.

parameters.name: content
parameters.description: The content to upload (use jsonStr.getBytes() for JSON)

parameters.name: request
parameters.description: Built with builder pattern: artifactReference (with categoryType and fileType), visibility

returns.description: artifactId ready for use in createAndStartTask

example:
String jsonStr = JsonUtils.serialize(artifact);
CreateArtifactUploadUrlRequest request = CreateArtifactUploadUrlRequest.builder()
    .artifactReference(ArtifactReference.builder()
        .artifactType(ArtifactType.builder()
            .categoryType(ArtifactCategoryType.HITL_FROM_AGENT)
            .fileType(FileType.JSON)
            .build())
        .build())
    .visibility(Visibility.INTERNAL)
    .build();
String artifactId = client.uploadArtifact(jsonStr.getBytes(), request);

name: JsonUtils
description: Final utility class for HITL artifact serialization and deserialization. Uses Jackson ObjectMapper internally. All methods are static.

methods.name: serialize
methods.signature: static String serialize(Object artifact) throws SerializationException
methods.description: Serialize any object to JSON with {"properties": {...}} wrapper. Works with schema-generated models or POJOs.

methods.name: deserialize
methods.signature: static <T> T deserialize(String json, Class<T> clazz) throws SerializationException
methods.description: Deserialize JSON with {"properties": {...}} wrapper to typed object. Unlike Python SDK, does NOT return a should_refresh flag -- check QT_REFRESH manually.

methods.name: getObjectMapper
methods.description: Get the shared Jackson ObjectMapper for manual operations (e.g. checking QT_REFRESH flag).

name: ArtifactHelper
description: Utility for uploading and downloading content via presigned S3 URLs. Uses java.net.http.HttpClient with 120-second default timeout.

parameters.name: httpClient
parameters.description: Custom HttpClient for testing. Default creates one with 120s timeout.

methods.name: upload
methods.signature: void upload(String presignedUrl, Map<String, List<String>> headers, byte[] content) throws IOException
methods.description: Upload content to presigned S3 URL.

methods.name: download
methods.signature: String download(String presignedUrl, Map<String, List<String>> headers) throws IOException
methods.description: Download artifact content as UTF-8 string from presigned S3 URL.

name: SerializationException
description: Checked exception thrown by JsonUtils.serialize() and JsonUtils.deserialize() when serialization or deserialization fails. Extends java.lang.Exception.
signature:
new SerializationException(String message)
new SerializationException(String message, Throwable cause)
new SerializationException(Throwable cause)

name: ComponentRegistry
description: Enum of all registered UX component IDs. Each value has a string value matching the uxComponentId used in CreateHitlTaskRequest. Use ComponentRegistry.containsComponentId(String) to validate.

methods.name: containsComponentId
methods.signature: static ComponentRegistry containsComponentId(String id) throws IllegalArgumentException
methods.description: Look up a ComponentRegistry by uxComponentId string. Throws if not found.

values.name: TABLE_COMPONENT
values.description: Data table with filtering and pagination

name: Java vs Python SDK Differences
description: Key differences between the Java and Python HITL SDKs for developers choosing between them or porting code.

differences.java: Generic <T> typed response via Class<T> parameter
AWS SDK v2 RequestContext model with builder
Enums (Severity, BlockingType, etc.) from AWS SDK v2 model package
differences.python: Enums defined in SDK itself (str, Enum)
